---
title: "Your Presentation Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle or Tagline"
author: "Author Name"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.css]
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard:
      enabled: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: "logo.png"
    footer: "Your Organization © 2024"
reveal-options:
  hash: true
  center: true
---

## Welcome to Your Presentation

This is your first slide. You can add content here.

::: {.notes}
Speaker notes go here. They won't appear in the presentation but you'll see them in presenter view.
:::

---

## Slide with Bullet Points

- First main point
  - Sub-point one
  - Sub-point two
- Second main point
- Third main point

---

## Two Column Layout

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Left Column

- Point 1
- Point 2
- Point 3
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Right Column

Text content or images can go here.

![](https://via.placeholder.com/300)
:::
:::

---

## Code Example

```python
# This is a code block with syntax highlighting
def hello_world():
    print("Hello, World!")
    return True

# Call the function
result = hello_world()
```

---

## Incremental Bullet Points

::: {.incremental}
- First point appears
- Then this one
- And finally this one
:::

---

## Lists and Fragments

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

1. Numbered list item 1
2. Numbered list item 2
3. Numbered list item 3

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

- Unordered item
- Another item
- Final item

:::

::::

---

## Tables

| Feature | Details | Status |
|---------|---------|--------|
| Responsive | Mobile-friendly | ✓ |
| Dark Mode | Built-in theme | ✓ |
| Code Highlighting | Syntax colors | ✓ |
| Animations | Smooth transitions | ✓ |

---

## Math Equations

Inline equation: $E = mc^2$

Display equation:

$$\frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$$

More complex mathematics can be easily integrated.

---

## Image with Caption

![A beautiful landscape image](https://via.placeholder.com/600x400)

*Figure 1: This is a caption for the image*

---

## Blockquote

> "The only way to do great work is to love what you do." 
> 
> — Steve Jobs

---

## Vertical Slides

You can navigate vertically within a section.

--- 

### Nested Vertical Slide

This slide is nested below the previous one. Use arrow keys to navigate.

---

## Multi-line Code Block

```r
# R code example
library(ggplot2)

data <- data.frame(
  x = 1:10,
  y = rnorm(10)
)

ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Callout Boxes

:::{.callout-note}
## Note
This is a note callout box for important information.
:::

:::{.callout-warning}
## Warning
This is a warning callout box for cautionary information.
:::

:::{.callout-tip}
## Tip
This is a tip callout box for helpful suggestions.
:::

---

## Highlighted Code

```{.python code-line-numbers="1-2|3|4"}
# Lines 1-2 highlight first
import pandas as pd
# Then line 3 highlights
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
# Finally line 4 highlights
print(df.head())
```

---

## Speaker Notes Example

This slide demonstrates speaker notes.

::: {.notes}
- Start with an engaging opener
- Emphasize the three main points
- Conclude with a call to action
- Allow 2-3 minutes for questions
:::

---

## Background Images

![](https://via.placeholder.com/1200x800){background-size="cover"}

### Title Over Background

---

## Custom Colors

::: {style="color: #e74c3c;"}
This text is in red.
:::

::: {style="color: #3498db; background-color: #ecf0f1; padding: 20px; border-radius: 10px;"}
This text is blue with a light background.
:::

---

## Emphasis and Formatting

- **Bold text** for emphasis
- *Italic text* for alternative emphasis
- ***Bold and italic*** for strong emphasis
- ~~Strikethrough text~~ for removed content
- `Code text` for inline code

---

## Lists with Pauses

- Point one

. . .

- Point two (appears after pause)

. . .

- Point three (appears after another pause)

---

## Final Slide

### Thank You!

Questions?

---

## Contact Information

- **Email:** your.email@example.com
- **Website:** www.yourwebsite.com
- **LinkedIn:** linkedin.com/in/yourprofile
- **GitHub:** github.com/yourprofile
